articles.title: Max Verstappen Thinks About Quitting Formula 1
articles.summary: Max Verstappen is a famous race car driver in Formula 1. He is thinking about leaving the sport because he is not having fun anymore. This season, there are new rules for the cars, and he does not like how they make him race. He finished eighth in a race in Japan and said he misses being with his family and friends. Even though he has won many championships before, he feels unhappy with the way things are now. He loves racing, but if it is not enjoyable, he might stop. This is a big decision for him because Formula 1 has been his passion since he was a kid.

articles.title: A Girl's Skin Problem and the Mystery Behind It
articles.summary: Bethany is a young woman who had eczema, a common skin condition, since she was a child. Doctors gave her steroid creams to help, and they worked for many years. But when she turned 18, her skin got much worse, becoming red, itchy, and painful all over her body. She felt very sick and couldn't do normal things like getting out of bed. Doctors didn't believe her and kept saying it was just eczema, but Bethany knew it was different. She and others share their stories online using the hashtag TSW, which stands for topical steroid withdrawal. This condition is not well understood, and some doctors don't know how to treat it. A professor named Sara Brown is studying TSW to learn more about why it happens and help people like Bethany.

articles.title: Former CEO's Phone Calls in Court Case
articles.summary: Mike Jeffries, a former boss of Abercrombie & Fitch, is in a court case about sex trafficking charges. He was recorded on phone calls saying he is in big trouble if he is found fit for trial. His lawyers say he has dementia and Alzheimer's disease, so he cannot stand trial. Prosecutors say he is focused and his health is better. The calls were made while he was in a mental health unit in prison. A judge will decide in May if he is fit for trial after hearing from doctors. Jeffries and two others are accused of running a sex trafficking business. They say they are not guilty.

articles.title: Iran Attacks Saudi Base and Hurts U.S. Soldiers
articles.summary: Iran attacked a Saudi Arabian air base with missiles and drones, hurting at least 15 U.S. troops, including five seriously. This happened on Friday, and it is part of a war that has injured over 300 American service members. More U.S. forces are coming to the Middle East to help, including a Navy ship with 2,500 Marines. The base is near Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, and is used by both Saudi and U.S. troops. Earlier in the week, there were other attacks on the same base that hurt more U.S. soldiers. The U.S. is sending ships and planes to the region to protect its troops and be ready for any problems. This shows that the conflict is getting bigger and more dangerous.

articles.title: Why Things Cost More: Gas and Shipping Problems
articles.summary: When the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran, it caused big problems for gas prices and how things get shipped around the world. This makes fuel more expensive for trucks, boats, and planes. Trucks use diesel fuel to carry food, toys, and other items to stores, so when diesel costs go up, prices in stores go up too. Also, attacks in Qatar stopped the production of natural gas, which is used to make plastics, packaging, and fertilizer. This means fewer supplies for making everyday things like bottles and boxes. Factories in other countries slow down because shipping is harder and energy costs more, leading to delays and higher prices for electronics and medicines. Air cargo is affected too, with some countries closing their airspace, making it tricky to deliver important goods quickly. All these issues mean you might pay more for things you buy.

articles.title: Arctic Ice Shrinks in Winter as Earth Gets Warmer
articles.summary: Arctic sea ice shrank to tie its lowest winter level ever measured. This is surprising because winter is when ice usually grows. The Earth is getting warmer, breaking heat records in many places like the United States, Mexico, and Australia. Arctic ice is important because it reflects sunlight, helping to keep the planet cool. Without enough ice, more heat goes into the oceans. This affects animals like polar bears and seals that need ice to live. Scientists say the ice is shrinking steadily due to warming temperatures. Less ice in winter means a head start for summer melting, which can lead to more extreme weather. The ice area was much smaller than the average from 1981 to 2010, about twice the size of Texas. Melting sea ice does not make sea levels rise, but it changes the climate.
